tasks.title: Group C - Benchmark Runner & Accuracy Gate
tasks.description: Build the benchmark runner, reporting surface, and CI accuracy gate that validate ScanMeNow detection against independent PHI/PII testing data. Task C consumes Task D extraction/OCR support, Task G PII capability, and Task H independent testing data.
tasks.notes: PII THRESHOLD POLICY (2026-05-27): Task C must define separate benchmark thresholds for each PII entity label instead of reusing PHI/HIPAA thresholds wholesale. Task G defines the activated PII label set, including CRYPTO and NRP; Task H supplies corpus coverage. Task C owns per-label PII recall/precision thresholds and should document any labels with no hard threshold or best-effort treatment. Thresholds are now written — read docs/benchmark_thresholds.json, which is the authoritative source the runner enforces. --- ARCHITECTURE NOTES (orchestrator, 2026-05-27): (1) EXTRACTION STACK: Task C's runner.py must import Task D's extraction stack directly (src/scanmenow/walker/reader.py) — do not duplicate extraction logic. Same code path = same extracted text = same offsets as corpus. (2) PDF LIBRARY: pdfplumber is authoritative for text-layer PDF extraction across the whole project. Task D uses it, Task H corpus was produced with it, Task C must inherit it via the shared reader. (3) CORPUS PATH: SCANMENOW_CORPUS_PATH env var sets corpus location; --corpus CLI arg overrides; pytest gate skips gracefully if env var unset (do not fail CI on missing corpus). (4) TOLERANCE: exact char offset match for text-native formats; ±5 char tolerance for OCR-derived formats (PNG/JPG/TIFF/BMP/scanned PDF). --- ARCHITECTURAL DEPENDENCY CONTRACTS (2026-05-27): Task C is a CONSUMER of three cross-task contracts. CONTRACT 1 — build_analyzer() [Owner: Task #3 + Task G (#9)]: import build_analyzer() from src/scanmenow/detection/analyzer.py and call it once per benchmark run. Do NOT re-register recognizers inside runner.py. Task G (#9) will have already merged all 8 PII recognizers into this factory before Task C runs; they appear automatically. CONTRACT 2 — read_file_text(path) [Owner: Task D (#5)]: import read_file_text(path: Path) -> str from src/scanmenow/walker/reader.py and use it for all corpus text extraction in the benchmark runner. Do NOT duplicate any format-handling logic — this is the hard coupling that keeps corpus golden-finding offsets consistent with production scanning offsets. CONTRACT 3 — docs/benchmark_thresholds.json [Owner: Task C (#4), this task]: the runner reads this file at startup; no thresholds are hardcoded in runner.py. Adding a new entity type means adding a row to the JSON; the runner picks it up with no code change. min_recall: null + hard_threshold: false = best-effort, reported only, never fails CI.

tasks.title: Group D - Filesystem Walker & Integration
tasks.description: Local filesystem walker with path resolution, skip patterns, symlink policy, max-depth limits, and end-to-end CLI integration. File format scope: TXT, CSV, JSON, PDF (pdfplumber), DOCX/XLSX/PPTX (python-docx/openpyxl/python-pptx), legacy DOC (LibreOffice headless subprocess), PNG/JPG/JPEG/TIFF/BMP/GIF (pytesseract + Tesseract OCR). System dependencies: Tesseract OCR, LibreOffice. Platform scope: Windows and Linux.
tasks.notes: Hardening concern carried from Task #3 dual review (Claude Sonnet 4.6 + Codex GPT-5, 2026-05-26): when the filesystem walker pipes findings through the detection layer, expand the recognizer collision tests from 'at least one entity fires' into expected entity/span precedence behaviour. A numeric token can match MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER, ACCOUNT_NUMBER, and DEVICE_IDENTIFIER simultaneously; if downstream redaction or reporting logic needs deterministic entity precedence over overlapping PHI spans, that dedup/priority contract must be defined and tested here rather than left implicit. OCR SCOPE ADDITION (2026-05-27): Task D now includes OCR capability for image-format files (PNG, JPG, JPEG, GIF) via Tesseract (pytesseract), and legacy .doc files via LibreOffice headless subprocess (cross-platform, no extra pip package). LibreOffice is a system dependency like Tesseract; absence triggers WARNING + skip rather than crash. Linux testing will be performed on WSL. INSTALLER NOTE: Task #12 (Dependency Bootstrapper) owns auto-detection and installation of Tesseract and LibreOffice for end users. --- ORCHESTRATOR CORRECTIONS (2026-05-27, cross-session sync from Task C/H planning): (1) PDF LIBRARY CORRECTION: use pdfplumber (not pdfminer.six) for text-layer PDF extraction. Task H corpus will generate .extracted.txt companions for text PDFs using pdfplumber per docs/GROUP-C-CORPUS-SPEC.md — if Task D uses pdfminer.six instead, extracted text will differ and all text-layer PDF corpus offsets will drift in the benchmark. Update reader.py to use pdfplumber, page-by-page extraction joined with newline. (2) IMAGE FORMAT GAP: add .tiff/.tif and .bmp to SUPPORTED_EXTENSIONS and reader format handlers. The Task H corpus spec (docs/GROUP-C-CORPUS-SPEC.md, correction #4 in Task H backlog notes commit 5e680077) requires doc_013_lab_result_scan.tiff and doc_014_consent_form_scan.bmp — these files will be in corpus/ and must be scannable. Both use the same pytesseract extraction path as PNG/JPG. Without them, two corpus documents produce zero findings and Task C benchmark shows 100% false-negative rate for all entity types in those files. --- ARCHITECTURAL DEPENDENCY CONTRACT (2026-05-27): Task D (#5) OWNS Contract 2. Task D must expose read_file_text(path: Path) -> str as a stable public function at src/scanmenow/walker/reader.py. Task C (benchmark runner) imports this function directly — it does not duplicate format-handling logic. Interface contract: returns extracted plain text (str); returns empty string + logs WARNING on graceful skip (missing Tesseract, missing LibreOffice); raises on unrecoverable errors. This is the hard coupling that keeps corpus golden-finding offsets consistent with production scanning offsets — any drift between Task D's extraction and Task C's extraction invalidates benchmark accuracy numbers in both directions. The extraction libraries and commands specified in docs/GROUP-C-CORPUS-SPEC.md are authoritative for this function.

objective.statement: Deliver a working filesystem walker that scans all supported file formats (TXT, CSV, JSON, PDF, DOCX, XLSX, PPTX, DOC, PNG, JPG, JPEG, TIFF, BMP, GIF) for PHI/PII, stores structured findings in SQLite with source_line provenance, and exposes `scanmenow scan <path>` as a working CLI command on Windows and Linux.
successCriteria: All 10 file format readers extract non-empty text from fixture files and pass unit tests
FileSystemWalker correctly applies skip patterns, max-depth, and symlink skip policy as verified by unit tests
Span collision test asserts ≥2 distinct entity types on overlapping spans; get_overlapping_findings() returns all
run_scan() persists findings in SQLite with source_line populated for text-based formats
scanmenow scan <path> CLI command exits 0, prints summary, and persists findings for a multi-format fixture directory
nonGoals: Dependency bootstrapper or auto-install of Tesseract/LibreOffice for end users (Task #12)
Streaming or chunked scanning for files >100MB

proofMap.criterion: All 10 file format readers extract non-empty text from fixture files and pass unit tests
FileSystemWalker correctly applies skip patterns, max-depth, and symlink skip policy as verified by unit tests
Span collision test asserts ≥2 distinct entity types on overlapping spans; get_overlapping_findings() returns all
run_scan() persists findings in SQLite with source_line populated for text-based formats
scanmenow scan <path> CLI command exits 0, prints summary, and persists findings for a multi-format fixture directory

stopConditions: Task #3 (HIPAA recognizers) is not yet merged to main — Task #5 branch must be based on task/3 branch or wait for merge before build can start
LibreOffice not installed on build machine — .doc tests must be mocked or skipped; document clearly in test

proofUnits.title: Walker unit tests pass
proofUnits.expectedBehavior: FileSystemWalker correctly applies skip patterns, max-depth, symlink skip, and hidden file policy across all configured test scenarios
proofUnits.expectedInitialFailure: ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'scanmenow.walker' or test file does not exist
proofUnits.expectedPassingEvidence: All walker tests pass — at minimum: skip pattern, max depth, symlink skip, hidden file, path resolution, unsupported extension tests green

proofUnits.title: Reader unit tests pass (all formats)
proofUnits.expectedBehavior: read_file_text() returns non-empty text for each supported format fixture; graceful skip (empty string + WARNING) when Tesseract or LibreOffice absent
proofUnits.expectedInitialFailure: ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'scanmenow.walker.reader' or test file does not exist
proofUnits.expectedPassingEvidence: All reader tests pass including graceful-skip mock tests for missing Tesseract/LibreOffice

proofUnits.title: Span collision contract tests
proofUnits.expectedBehavior: A numeric token prefixed with MRN, account, and serial context keywords produces ≥2 distinct entity_type findings with overlapping spans; get_overlapping_findings() returns all of them
proofUnits.expectedPassingEvidence: test_overlapping_entities_all_saved and test_get_overlapping_findings_returns_all pass; hardening contract comment present in test source

proofUnits.title: Integration scan test — fixture directory to SQLite findings
proofUnits.expectedBehavior: run_scan() on the tests/fixtures/ directory produces a ScanSummary with files_scanned >= 3, persists findings in SQLite, source_line is populated for text-based format findings, source_file is set
proofUnits.expectedInitialFailure: ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'scanmenow.scanner' or test file does not exist
proofUnits.expectedPassingEvidence: All scanner integration tests pass; findings retrievable from SQLite after scan

proofUnits.title: CLI scan command end-to-end
proofUnits.expectedBehavior: scanmenow scan ./tests/fixtures/ exits 0, prints a summary table with files scanned and findings count, and a ScanJob record exists in SQLite after the run
proofUnits.expectedInitialFailure: Error: No such command 'scan' (Typer exits with code 2)
proofUnits.expectedPassingEvidence: Exit 0, summary output visible in terminal, job_id printed, findings retrievable from DB

tasks.title: Group F - Phase 1 Exit Gate & Verification
tasks.description: Accuracy threshold validation, CLI functional tests on real fixture directory, documentation, and Phase 1 completion (1 subtask)

tasks.title: Group E - Cloud & Retention Services
tasks.description: Firestore connection layer with environment flag for local dev, retention policy evaluation, and cloud integration (4 subtasks)

tasks.title: Task G - Add PII to Scope and Capability
tasks.description: Add PII scanning to the project scope and detection capability alongside PHI scanning. Task G defines the expanded PII entity set that Task C must benchmark and Task H must represent in independent testing data.
tasks.notes: TASK G BUILD NOTES (orchestrator, 2026-05-27): LOCKED PII LABEL SET — 8 US-focused Presidio built-in types: CREDIT_CARD, US_BANK_NUMBER, US_PASSPORT, US_DRIVER_LICENSE, IBAN_CODE, CRYPTO, MEDICAL_LICENSE, NRP. International PII (AU, UK, SG, EU) deferred to Task #16. No custom recognizers needed — all 8 are Presidio built-ins. --- PHASE 1 GATE (critical): before writing any code, audit the installed Presidio package to verify exact recognizer class names and entity type strings. Wrong class names cause silent misses — the integration test (engine.get_recognizers()) is the verification gate. --- REQUIRED DELIVERABLES: (1) src/scanmenow/detection/recognizers/pii/__init__.py + us_pii.py — exports ALL_PII_RECOGNIZERS; (2) src/scanmenow/detection/recognizers/__init__.py updated — merges ALL_PII_RECOGNIZERS into ALL_RECOGNIZERS; (3) docs/pii_coverage.json — 8-entry registry mirroring hipaa_coverage.json format: entity_type, Presidio recognizer class, confidence notes, jurisdiction, collision_risk; (4) docs/registries/entity-types.md — 8 new entries following existing format (Task H coverage matrix depends on this being up to date before corpus production starts); (5) tests/test_pii_recognizers.py — positive + negative per type (16 tests min) + integration test confirming all 8 registered. --- COLLISION DOCUMENTATION (required in pii_coverage.json): US_DRIVER_LICENSE may overlap with CERTIFICATE_LICENSE_NUMBER; MEDICAL_LICENSE may overlap with MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER. Both are expected and Presidio handles them — document collision_risk field per entity. --- NRP NOTE: Presidio NRP recognizer detects nationality/religion/political group mentions in text (e.g. 'identifies as Catholic', 'US citizen', 'Republican Party member'). It is keyword/phrase detection, not a structured ID format. Document as best-effort in pii_coverage.json. Task C assigns NRP no hard accuracy threshold (same as BIOMETRIC_IDENTIFIER). --- DOWNSTREAM DEPENDENCIES: Task H corpus production is waiting on entity-types.md to be updated before it can finalize its coverage matrix. Task C benchmark thresholds for all 8 PII types are already written in docs/benchmark_thresholds.json — read it for context. --- ARCHITECTURAL DEPENDENCY CONTRACT (2026-05-27): Task G (#9) OWNS Contract 1. All 8 PII recognizers must be registered into build_analyzer() in src/scanmenow/detection/analyzer.py — NOT into a separate factory or alternate entry point. build_analyzer() is the single factory for the AnalyzerEngine. Task C imports build_analyzer() as-is and calls it once per benchmark run; it does not know about PII recognizers specifically. Any recognizer Task G merges into ALL_RECOGNIZERS is automatically benchmarked by Task C on the next run with no code change in Task C. This means Task G must merge into src/scanmenow/detection/recognizers/__init__.py (ALL_RECOGNIZERS list) before Task C's first full benchmark run. Do not expose a separate build_pii_analyzer() or similar — Task C will not call it.

tasks.title: Task H - Independent Testing Data for Task C
tasks.description: Produce the independent synthetic PHI/PII testing corpus, companion extracted text files, golden findings JSON, near-miss decoys, and delivery notes that Task C will consume for validation, accuracy benchmarking, and regression guarding. This task must preserve independence by avoiding ScanMeNow source code, tests, and existing fixtures while authoring the corpus.
tasks.notes: PII ENTITY LABEL SET (from Task G, 2026-05-27): Task H corpus must include representative examples for all 8 US PII entity types activated by Task G: CREDIT_CARD (Luhn-validated card numbers), US_BANK_NUMBER (US routing/account numbers), US_PASSPORT (US passport numbers), US_DRIVER_LICENSE (US state driver's license numbers), IBAN_CODE (SWIFT/IBAN format bank account numbers), CRYPTO (cryptocurrency wallet addresses — Bitcoin, Ethereum, etc.), MEDICAL_LICENSE (medical license and NPI numbers), NRP (nationality/religion/political group mentions). These supplement the 15 PHI HIPAA Safe Harbor entity types already in scope. TOTAL LABEL SET: 23 distinct entity labels. --- SPEC CORRECTIONS AND CLARIFICATIONS (2026-05-27, from Task C planning session — read docs/GROUP-C-CORPUS-SPEC.md for full detail): (1) COVERAGE REQUIREMENT CORRECTION: the '2 positive examples per PII type' figure in the original note is superseded — correct requirement is >=3 findings across >=2 documents for all 21 hard-threshold labels. BIOMETRIC_IDENTIFIER and NRP require only >=2 mentions with no hard accuracy threshold (best-effort, reported only). (2) THRESHOLD FILE: per-label recall thresholds are now in docs/benchmark_thresholds.json — this is the authoritative source the benchmark runner enforces. Read it before writing the corpus. (3) MEDICAL_LICENSE vs CERTIFICATE_LICENSE_NUMBER OVERLAP: NPI numbers and state medical license numbers should appear as MEDICAL_LICENSE in golden_findings.json only — do not add CERTIFICATE_LICENSE_NUMBER for the same span even if that recognizer would also fire. (4) MINIMUM DOCUMENTS: 15 documents minimum across all 13 required formats: txt, csv, docx, doc, xlsx, xls, pptx, pdf (text-layer), pdf (image-only/scanned), png, jpg, tiff, bmp. (5) OCR EXTRACTION: all image files and image-only PDFs must be extracted with pytesseract, lang='eng', default PSM, exactly as shown in docs/GROUP-C-CORPUS-SPEC.md — offsets in golden_findings.json reference the OCR-extracted text, not the raw image bytes. (6) COMPANION FILES: every non-plaintext, non-CSV document needs a .extracted.txt companion file produced by the library specified in the spec for that format (python-docx for .docx, LibreOffice headless for .doc, openpyxl for .xlsx, xlrd for .xls, python-pptx for .pptx, pdfplumber for text-layer PDF, pytesseract for images and scanned PDF). (7) NEAR-MISS DECOYS: at least 2 near-miss decoy entries per document recorded in the decoys[] array of golden_findings.json — not just avoided in the document text.

tasks.title: Build Bi-Temporal Progress Tracking
tasks.description: Add append-only task history so backlog changes can be queried by both project-effective time and system-recorded time while preserving docs/backlog.json as the current-state projection.

tasks.title: Dependency Bootstrapper - First-Run System Dependency Installer
tasks.description: On first Electron app launch, detect whether required system dependencies are installed (Tesseract OCR, LibreOffice headless) and silently install any that are missing. Separate install flows for Windows and Linux. Covers: version detection, silent download/install, progress feedback to the user, graceful failure with actionable error message if install is blocked (e.g. no internet, insufficient permissions). Must handle partial installs and re-check on subsequent launches. Option to bundle installers within the Electron package vs. stream from CDN on first run — decision affects package size vs. install reliability.
tasks.notes: System dependencies introduced by Task #5: Tesseract OCR (image scanning), LibreOffice headless (legacy .doc extraction). Both are system-level binaries, not pip packages. Windows install paths: Tesseract at C:\Program Files\Tesseract-OCR\tesseract.exe (winget: UB-Mannheim.TesseractOCR), LibreOffice at C:\Program Files\LibreOffice\program\soffice.exe. Linux install: apt-get install tesseract-ocr libreoffice (or equivalent). WSL-aware: distinguish WSL from native Linux for path resolution.

tasks.title: Windows Installer Package
tasks.description: Build a standalone Windows installer (.exe or .msi) for the ScanMeNow Electron app using Electron Builder. Package includes: embedded Python runtime (PyInstaller or uv-bundled), all pip dependencies, and the dependency bootstrapper (Task #12) that detects and installs Tesseract and LibreOffice on first run. Target: Windows 10/11 64-bit. Deliverable: signed installer that passes Windows SmartScreen, installs to Program Files, adds Start Menu entry and optional desktop shortcut.
tasks.notes: Electron Builder target: nsis (NSIS installer) or msi. Python embedding strategy TBD: PyInstaller bundled alongside Electron, or subprocess bridge to system Python. Code signing required for SmartScreen clearance. Consider auto-update via electron-updater.

tasks.title: Linux Installer Package
tasks.description: Build standalone Linux installer packages for the ScanMeNow Electron app. Targets: DEB (Ubuntu/Debian), RPM (RHEL/CentOS/Fedora), and AppImage (distribution-agnostic). Package includes: embedded Python runtime, all pip dependencies, and the dependency bootstrapper (Task #12) that detects and installs Tesseract and LibreOffice via the system package manager (apt, dnf, yum). Primary test environment: WSL (Ubuntu). Deliverable: three package artifacts and an install script.
tasks.notes: Electron Builder targets: deb, rpm, AppImage. WSL testing confirmed by Scott (2026-05-27). System package manager detection: prefer apt, fallback to dnf/yum/zypper. Bootstrapper must handle headless Linux installs (no GUI sudo prompt). Desktop entry (.desktop file) for GUI environments.

tasks.title: VMware VM Deployment Package
tasks.description: Build a pre-configured Linux VM image in OVF/OVA format with ScanMeNow fully installed and all system dependencies (Tesseract, LibreOffice) pre-configured. Targeted at enterprise deployments where application isolation, air-gapped environments, or IT-managed VM provisioning is required. Base OS: Ubuntu LTS (latest stable). Deliverable: OVA file importable into VMware vSphere, VMware Workstation, and VMware Fusion. Includes first-boot configuration script for network, hostname, and initial admin credentials.
tasks.notes: Base image: Ubuntu 24.04 LTS (minimal server). VM spec: 4 vCPU, 8GB RAM, 60GB disk (minimum). VMware Tools pre-installed. ScanMeNow installed via Linux DEB package (Task #14 artifact). Consider also producing a VirtualBox OVA variant. Air-gap support: all dependency installers pre-bundled, no internet required post-deploy.

tasks.title: International PII Scope Expansion
tasks.description: Extend PII detection coverage beyond US-focused entity types to include international and GDPR-scoped identifiers. Activate Presidio's country-specific built-in recognizers: UK NHS number, AU TFN/Medicare/ABN/ACN, SG NRIC/FIN, EU fiscal codes (IT, ES, FI, PL), and any others supported by Presidio at implementation time. Produce a companion pii_international_coverage.json registry documenting each identifier, jurisdiction, recognizer source, and confidence notes. Depends on Task #9 (US PII foundation) being production.
tasks.notes: Deferred from Task #9 scope decision (2026-05-27): Scott confirmed US-only PII scope for Task #9. International expansion added as a separate backlog item for future consideration. No custom recognizers expected — all international types are Presidio built-ins requiring only registration.
